figures.caption: Figure 1: Problem setup and specialized one-block transformer model. (a) Action token xj selects permutation σxj , which maps current state xℓ to next state xℓ+1. (b) Teacher sequence from actions x1, . . . , xL and initial state xL+1; for i = L+ 1, . . . , 2L, xi+1 is obtained by applying xi−L to xi. (c) Student prediction: RoPE attention retrieves the action at fixed lag −L, and the logic block applies the corresponding weighted sum of learnable matrices to the one-hot state, producing logits zℓ+1.

Figure 2: Dynamics of the order parameters A, R, and S in panels a), b), and c), respectively. Simulations are averaged over 100 seeds, and σ denotes the standard deviation. The theoretical predictions agree very well with the simulations (note different scale in panel c)).
Table 1: Hyperparameters for the training simulations.
Figure 5: Entries of the attention block as a function of the learning time.

Figure 6: (a) Variances for the mean correct and other logits during training. (b) Simulated rollout accuracy for the empirical logit means and variances, where the empirical and theoretically predicted logit means had a near-perfect agreement.
Table 2: Different set of hyperparameters for training simulations.
Figure 7: Order parameters from theory and simulations.
Figure 8: Next-token accuracy and final rollout accuracy for logit variances held fixed at their initial values of σ2 k = 0.0466, σ2 k = 0.0467.
